Test Scenario Description: Verify successful user registration with valid data and bcrypt password hashing
Verify user login creates JWT session and sets HttpOnly cookie
Verify rate-limiting: 429 Too Many Requests returned after 10 failed logins within 1 minute (Redis)
Verify Concurrent Sessions: Logging in from a second device invalidates the first session's JWT
Verify semantic search (Pinecone) for 'laptop bag' successfully returns 'backpacks' via vector embeddings
Verify Redis Caching: /products API responds in <50ms on 2nd request (Cache HIT) vs >200ms on 1st request (Cache MISS)
Verify filtering products by dynamic facets (Category, Price range, Rating)
Verify cart data persists accurately in Redis across page reloads and browser sessions
Verify Stripe webhook successfully updates order status to 'Processing' upon successful charge
Verify Boundary Condition: Checkout with amount $999,999.99 handles precision errors correctly
Verify Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) failover: If Order Confirmation Email fails to send, message is requeued in RabbitMQ
Verify Database Consistency: Order Total exactly matches the sum of individual item prices + tax + shipping in PostgreSQL
Verify RBAC: Non-Admin user gets 403 Forbidden when attempting to access /admin routes
Verify concurrent inventory update: Two users buying the last 1 item simultaneously does not result in negative inventory (Race Condition check)
Verify Responsive UI Behavior: Checkout and Cart workflows render correctly on mobile viewports (390px width) without horizontal scrolling
Verify Token Expiry: Attempting an API request 61 minutes after login (with a 1h TTL token) returns 401 Unauthorized and prompts re-login
Verify Checkout Interruption: Dropping network connection precisely after Stripe token generation but before order submission does not charge the user
Verify Nodemailer Email Automation: Successful order triggers formatted HTML email receipt delivered to the user's registered address
